title: A survey of industrial model predictive control technology
relevance: Must-read classic: commercial MPC vendors review (Honeywell Aspen Shell) - baseline to justify AVEVA APC + ANPOS hybrid
aiSummary: Qin and Badgwell catalogue the algorithmic foundations and performance benchmarks of commercial MPC products—including Honeywell and Aspen—giving your team a precise technical baseline against which to measure the in-house ensemble. Use their documented constraint-handling and disturbance-rejection specifications as acceptance criteria when designing head-to-head KPIs. The survey also exposes where vendor implementations diverge from academic theory, revealing exploitable gaps where ML flexibility could outperform rigid QP-based controllers and justify eliminating licensing costs.

title: Constrained Model Predictive Control: Stability and Optimality
relevance: Foundational MPC stability theory with constraints - reference for increment limits (1%/hr feeders 1C/hr temperatures)
aiSummary: Implement the terminal constraint and terminal cost framework from Mayne et al. to guarantee closed-loop stability when your ensemble ML controller enforces the 1%/hr feeder and 1°C/hr temperature increment limits as hard MPC constraints. This theoretical grounding lets you defend the in-house build against vendor claims by demonstrating recursive feasibility, not just empirical performance. Use the stability proofs to set your prediction horizon length and tuning penalty weights before head-to-head benchmarking begins.

title: Economic Assessment of Advanced Process Control - A Survey and Framework
relevance: Framework for APC ROI measurement with 60+ expert survey - business case reference for ANPOS
aiSummary: Use Bauer & Craig's validated ROI framework to define the financial metrics—variance reduction, throughput gain, energy savings—before running the head-to-head experiment, ensuring both the in-house ensemble and vendor APC are judged on identical economic terms. The 60+ expert survey quantifies typical APC benefit ranges, giving the project a credible benchmark against which to size the licensing cost that elimination must offset. Adopting this framework early prevents post-hoc disputes about whether observed performance differences actually translate into business value.

title: Deep Learning-based Sequence Modeling for Advanced Process Control in Semiconductor Manufacturing
relevance: LSTM/TCN/1DCNN for APC - architectural reference for MAI advisory engine (already in project knowledge)
aiSummary: Adopt LSTM, TCN, and 1D-CNN as candidate sequence models within the in-house ensemble, since this paper benchmarks all three architectures against APC tasks structurally similar to yours. Train and compare these architectures on your fab's time-series process data to identify which delivers the best run-to-run control accuracy before committing to an ensemble configuration. A validated architectural shortlist directly sharpens the head-to-head experiment against the vendor system and reduces exploratory build time.

title: Reinforcement Learning in Process Industries: Review and Perspective
relevance: Holistic RL review across process control hierarchy - soft sensor low/high-level control FDD scheduling
aiSummary: Benchmark RL-based controllers against your in-house ensemble to establish whether learned policies outperform both vendor APC and your ML stack before committing to either licensing or build costs. The review's coverage of the full process control hierarchy—from soft sensors through scheduling—maps directly onto the layers your ensemble must replicate or beat. Mine its FDD and high-level control sections to identify failure modes that pure ensemble methods miss, sharpening your experimental design and success criteria.

title: A review on reinforcement learning: Introduction and applications in industrial process control
relevance: Tutorial-style paper explains RL fundamentals with process control case studies - ideal team onboarding material
aiSummary: Use this paper to onboard team members who lack RL foundations before they engage with the APC build, since its tutorial structure and process-control case studies translate directly to your industrial setting. It equips engineers to evaluate whether RL-based control strategies should complement or replace components of your ensemble ML stack when benchmarking against vendor APC. Reference its case studies to define reward functions and control objectives that mirror your own process metrics during the head-to-head experiment.

title: Reinforcement Learning - Overview of recent progress and implications for process control
relevance: Analysis of DRL suitability for process control - criteria: intelligent consistent offset-free nominally stable
aiSummary: Deep reinforcement learning struggles to guarantee offset-free control and nominal stability without additional engineering constraints, which means your in-house ensemble ML approach must explicitly incorporate these properties rather than assuming a learned policy will satisfy them. Evaluate your ensemble against the DRL suitability criteria—intelligent adaptation, consistent offset rejection, and stability guarantees—as a structured benchmark before the head-to-head experiment. Use this paper's framework to identify where your ensemble outperforms both DRL and vendor APC on these criteria, strengthening the cost-elimination business case.

title: Reinforcement learning approach to autonomous PID tuning
relevance: DRL for PID gains tuning - aligns with PID Gain Decomposition chapter in SRS v1.1
aiSummary: Deep reinforcement learning can autonomously tune PID gains without manual effort, offering a directly transferable method for the in-house APC build's PID Gain Decomposition module. Implement the RL-based tuner as a competing candidate against the ensemble ML stack to test whether adaptive gain scheduling closes the performance gap with vendor APC. Benchmark both approaches on the same plant loops to quantify whether autonomous PID tuning alone justifies eliminating licensing costs or must be combined with the broader ensemble.

title: Machine learning for industrial sensing and control: A survey and practical perspective
relevance: Industry practitioner perspective - soft sensor virtual metrology MPC+ML integration

title: Where Reinforcement Learning Meets Process Control: Review and Guidelines
relevance: Guidelines for offline training then transfer learning then online deployment - matches ANPOS deployment plan
aiSummary: Adopt the paper's staged deployment protocol—offline RL training on historical process data, transfer learning to close the sim-to-real gap, then cautious online rollout—as the integration roadmap for the in-house ensemble controller. This sequence directly de-risks the head-to-head experiment against the vendor APC by ensuring the ensemble is process-hardened before live comparison begins. Use the authors' guidelines to define explicit go/no-go criteria at each transition gate, giving the team defensible evidence that the in-house build can sustain control performance without vendor licensing.

title: Data-Reconciliation Based Fault-Tolerant Model Predictive Control for a Biomass Boiler
relevance: KPA Unicon BioGrate MPC using data reconciliation for flue gas O2 sensor fault - critical since O2 SP Bias is main MV for ANPOS
aiSummary: Embed data reconciliation as a pre-processing layer before your ensemble ML controller to detect and correct faulty O2 sensor readings, since corrupted O2 signals directly corrupt the primary manipulated variable and will invalidate any head-to-head comparison. This paper demonstrates that reconciling mass and energy balances around the flue-gas path catches sensor drift that standard filtering misses, giving your in-house controller the same fault-tolerance that vendor APC packages typically bundle. Replicating this layer in-house removes a key vendor differentiator and strengthens the legitimacy of your cost-elimination argument.

title: Model based control of a small-scale biomass boiler
relevance: MPC + emission model for biomass grate furnace - reduces CO 4x at partial load handles fuel moisture variation
aiSummary: Embed a physics-derived CO emission sub-model inside your ensemble ML controller to target emissions directly rather than treating them as a secondary constraint. This paper demonstrates that model-based control of combustion air staging cuts CO fourfold at partial load while compensating for fuel moisture variation—exactly the disturbance regimes where vendor APC typically justifies its cost. Replicating that emission-model structure in your in-house build strengthens the head-to-head case by attacking the vendor's strongest performance argument on measurable, regulatory-relevant grounds.

title: Development of feed-forward model predictive control for applications in biomass bubbling fluidized bed boilers
relevance: FF MPC handles biomass property variation (moisture heating value) - PI vs FF PI vs MPC vs FF MPC comparison
aiSummary: Feed-forward MPC outperforms reactive-only MPC when unmeasured disturbances (biomass moisture, heating value) are characterized ahead of time, demonstrating that pairing a predictive disturbance model with a controller delivers measurable setpoint-tracking gains over baseline PI. Your in-house ensemble ML can replicate this feed-forward layer by predicting fuel quality shifts from available sensor signals, feeding corrections into your APC loop before disturbances propagate. Benchmark your ensemble's disturbance-prediction latency and accuracy directly against the paper's FF MPC improvement margins to quantify whether in-house ML matches the vendor's built-in feed-forward capability.

title: Prediction Control of Biomass Combustion Boiler based on Multilayer Perceptron Neural Network
relevance: MLP prediction control - 92% accuracy for biomass direct-fired boiler similar to MPK/MPL structure
aiSummary: An MLP-based predictive controller achieving 92% accuracy on a biomass direct-fired boiler demonstrates that a single neural network layer can meet APC-grade control performance without vendor tooling. Replicate this architecture as a baseline component within your ensemble to anchor combustion-side predictions before stacking additional estimators. The similarity to your MPK/MPL boiler structure means transfer of hyperparameter ranges and training data schema should require minimal adaptation, reducing prototyping time for the head-to-head benchmark.

title: BoilerNet: Deep reinforcement learning-based combustion optimization network for pulverized coal boiler
relevance: Digital twin + TR-CNN + SAC agent for combustion optimization - reference for future closed-loop roadmap
aiSummary: Adopt BoilerNet's Soft Actor-Critic closed-loop control architecture as a future upgrade target once the ensemble ML baseline beats vendor APC, giving the project a concrete next-rung roadmap. The TR-CNN surrogate acting as a digital twin demonstrates how to replace costly live plant trials with simulated rollouts, directly reducing experimental risk during head-to-head testing. Benchmark the ensemble model's combustion predictions against the TR-CNN structure to quantify the accuracy gap the team must close before reinforcement learning becomes viable.

title: Image-based deep neural network prediction of the heat output of a step-grate biomass boiler
relevance: Flame imaging + DNN for multi-step-ahead prediction (30 min) on 3MW step-grate biomass - scales well to MPK Boiler 03
aiSummary: Flame-image CNN inputs feeding a DNN achieved 30-minute-ahead heat-output prediction on a 3 MW step-grate boiler, demonstrating that vision-based features can replace or augment conventional sensor streams in biomass combustion control. Replicate this image-plus-process-data fusion architecture within your in-house ensemble to give MPK Boiler 03 a predictive horizon that vendor APC systems rarely expose directly. If the ensemble matches vendor prediction accuracy at that horizon, you gain a concrete, cost-justified argument for eliminating the licensing fee.

title: Artificial Neural Networks and Experimental Data Analysis-Based Biomass Combustion Machine Dynamical Model Identification
relevance: MLP vs RNN vs LSTM vs GRU for biomass combustion dynamics - GRU wins with loss under 6%
aiSummary: GRU networks outperformed MLP, RNN, and LSTM architectures for capturing combustion dynamics with loss under 6%, so prioritize GRU as the recurrent backbone when building your in-house ensemble's dynamic process model. Train the GRU directly on plant historian data to replicate the physics the vendor APC encodes in proprietary models. This benchmarking methodology—comparing architectures head-to-head on real combustion data—gives your team a replicable validation framework to justify replacing licensed software with measurable performance parity.

title: Optimizing the Controlling Parameters of a Biomass Boiler Based on Big Data
relevance: K-means + FOD + BP-NN + ACO for biomass boiler 130 t/h (near MPK/MPL 170 TPH) - excellent blueprint
aiSummary: The paper demonstrates that combining K-means clustering, fractional-order differentiation, a BP neural network, and ant colony optimization directly improves combustion efficiency on a 130 t/h biomass boiler, providing a replicable ensemble architecture your team can implement in-house. Adapt this four-component pipeline to the 170 TPH MPK/MPL unit to benchmark against the vendor APC under matched operating conditions. The published parameter-optimization results give concrete performance baselines that justify whether eliminating licensing costs is achievable without sacrificing control quality.

title: Biomass Combustion Control in Small and Medium-Scale Boilers Based on Low Cost Sensing the Trend of Carbon Monoxide Emissions
relevance: CO-based combustion control - CO/O2 correlation data supports AHEGT and CO monitoring in FDS
aiSummary: CO concentration trends provide a low-cost, fast-feedback signal for combustion quality that can replace or complement slower O2-based control loops—train your ensemble models on CO trend features rather than point values to capture dynamic combustion states. The paper's CO/O2 correlation data directly supports feature engineering for the in-house APC, giving you vendor-comparable sensor inputs without proprietary hardware. Validating these features against your FDS and AHEGT signals strengthens the ensemble's interpretability case when benchmarking against the vendor system.

title: Experimental and analytical evaluation of NOX emissions in bagasse boilers
relevance: NOx in bagasse-fired boilers (Monte Alegre sugar mill Brazil) - directly relevant to MitrPhol context
aiSummary: Lora et al. quantify NOx formation mechanisms and influencing combustion variables in bagasse boilers, providing labeled physical relationships that can directly seed feature engineering for the ensemble ML model. The Brazil mill dataset establishes realistic NOx ranges and operational boundaries against which the in-house model's predictions should be validated, reducing reliance on vendor-supplied domain knowledge. Grounding the ensemble in this analytical framework strengthens the technical case that in-house APC can match vendor accuracy without recurring licensing cost.

title: PID Controllers: Theory Design and Tuning (2nd Edition)
relevance: PID bible - foundation of PID Gain Decomposition (P/I/D roles and tuning methods: Ziegler-Nichols lambda-tuning IMC)
aiSummary: Use Åström and Hägglund's gain decomposition framework to benchmark how your ensemble ML controller replicates the distinct regulatory roles of proportional, integral, and derivative action, giving you an interpretable diagnostic when the model underperforms vendor APC. Apply their Ziegler-Nichols and IMC lambda-tuning methods as baseline setpoint-response targets against which ensemble predictions must compete. This grounding ensures the in-house build is evaluated on established control-theoretic criteria, not just data-fit metrics, strengthening the business case for eliminating licensing costs.

title: Convex Optimization
relevance: Convex optimization bible - QP/SOCP/SDP formulations Lagrangian duality interior-point methods (MPC online solver foundation)
aiSummary: Use Boyd & Vandenberghe's interior-point method formulations to implement a lean, royalty-free QP or SOCP solver that drives the in-house MPC layer within the ensemble APC stack, directly replacing the vendor's embedded optimizer. Apply Lagrangian duality to derive tight constraint bounds and warm-start strategies that keep online solve times within scan-cycle budgets. This eliminates the black-box solver licensing cost that the vendor APC bundles, giving the project a concrete, auditable optimization core to benchmark against vendor performance.

title: N4SID: Subspace algorithms for the identification of combined deterministic-stochastic systems
relevance: Subspace ID algorithm - state-space model construction from I/O data matches AVEVA APC step testing workflow

title: System Identification: Theory for the User (2nd Edition)
relevance: Reference text for system identification - PEM (Prediction Error Method) ARX/ARMAX/OE/BJ model structures
aiSummary: Use PEM-based structures (ARX, ARMAX, OE, BJ) as interpretable baseline models against which to benchmark your ensemble ML predictions, since they provide uncertainty-quantified transfer-function estimates that expose process dynamics your black-box models must match or exceed. Fit these parametric models on your historical plant data to generate structured residuals and noise models that can directly inform ensemble feature engineering and input lag selection. Ljung's identifiability and variance-bias analysis also gives you a principled framework for diagnosing whether performance gaps in your head-to-head experiment stem from model structure, data quality, or experiment design.

title: Fast Model Predictive Control Using Online Optimization
venue: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology 18(2) 267-278
relevance: MPC QP with interior-point method in 5ms - suitable for ANPOS advisory engine real-time constraints
aiSummary: Embed a warm-started interior-point QP solver into the ANPOS advisory engine to meet the 5 ms real-time constraint without vendor MPC infrastructure. Wang and Boyd demonstrate that by pre-factoring constraint matrices offline and exploiting MPC's fixed problem structure, online solve time drops dramatically—directly enabling the in-house ensemble's predictions to feed a fast optimization layer. This eliminates a core argument for vendor APC licensing by replicating the real-time control computation internally at negligible marginal cost.

title: Stability and Stabilization: Discontinuities and the Effect of Disturbances
relevance: Input-to-State Stability (ISS) Control Lyapunov Functions - foundation for nonlinear stability analysis
aiSummary: ISS Control Lyapunov Functions provide a mathematically rigorous way to certify that a control system remains stable under bounded disturbances, which directly informs how your ensemble ML controller should handle process noise and model uncertainty in APC. Apply ISS theory to set explicit disturbance-rejection thresholds your in-house models must meet before declaring parity with the vendor system. This gives the head-to-head experiment a principled stability criterion beyond raw KPI metrics, strengthening the technical case for eliminating licensing costs.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Adaptive Control for Nonlinear Systems with Nonlinearities and Faults Using Fuzzy Logic and Neural Network Techniques
relevance: Modern review - input saturation dead-zone backlash (relevant to FD Fan valve feeder speed nonlinearities)
abstract: This review presents a comprehensive study of adaptive control techniques for nonlinear systems influenced by complex nonlinearities and system faults. Nonlinear systems are categorized into general, stochastic, and switched classes, with a focus on their modeling and control challenges. Common nonlinearities such as input saturation, dead-zone, and backlash-like hysteresis, along with actuator and sensor faults, are examined due to their critical impact on system performance. Fuzzy logic systems and neural networks are explored as effective function approximators capable of handling system uncertainties and complex dynamics. Their design methodologies, advantages, and implementation issues are discussed in detail. The review also highlights recent developments in fault-tolerant adaptive control using these intelligent approximators. Finally, the paper outlines open challenges and future research directions, including the integration of adaptive learning frameworks with real-time control and enhanced fault detection strategies for practical nonlinear systems.

title: Programmable Logic Controllers in the Context of Industry 4.0
relevance: Critical analysis of IEC 61131-3 + PLC limitations + Industry 4.0 directions - context for DCS-advisory integration
aiSummary: Leverage the paper's critique of IEC 61131-3 scan-cycle constraints to justify why ensemble ML inference must be decoupled from PLC execution loops and hosted on edge or cloud compute instead. The Industry 4.0 directions it outlines—OPC-UA connectivity, containerized logic, soft-PLC architectures—provide a concrete integration roadmap for pushing model outputs back to the DCS without proprietary vendor middleware. This directly strengthens the cost-elimination argument by showing the control layer can accept in-house APC signals through open standards, removing the technical dependency that sustains licensing fees.

title: IEC 61131-3: Programming Industrial Automation Systems - Concepts and Programming Languages
relevance: Definitive reference for IEC 61131-3 (LD FBD ST SFC) - essential for team coordination with Kishor on FDS implementation
aiSummary: Implement the IEC 61131-3 Structured Text (ST) and Function Block Diagram (FBD) standards defined here when coding the APC control logic that your ensemble ML outputs will drive on the PLC layer. This reference gives Kishor's team a shared specification for the Functional Design Specification, preventing integration failures between the ML inference layer and the automation hardware. Treat it as the contractual interface document ensuring vendor APC and in-house ensemble outputs translate identically into executable PLC instructions during head-to-head comparison.

title: Design of Robust Adaptive Fuzzy Controller for a Class of SISO Uncertain Nonlinear Systems
relevance: T-S fuzzy + Dynamic Surface Control for SISO uncertain nonlinear systems - handles fuel bulk density variation (mixed vs bagasse)
aiSummary: Dynamic Surface Control layered over T-S fuzzy models provides a ready-made architecture for handling the plant nonlinearity and bulk-density disturbances your ensemble ML controller must rival. Adapt this paper's adaptive law derivation to bound modeling error when bagasse moisture shifts alter fuel density, replacing vendor APC's gain-scheduling with a provably stable online update. Use the stability proof as a benchmark to validate that your in-house ensemble meets equivalent robustness guarantees before the head-to-head trial.
